channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

need to dump then pump to new moon!

62420765

yeah, they're building a wendy's near me

yeah whatever faggot

62421882

62423468

item.title: Well since it's becoming apparent all debt payers are suckers, what are the primary means of combating collapsing good g… — 19 replies — 76.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Well since it's becoming apparent all debt payers are suckers, what are the primary means of combating collapsing good g…

Open thread • Replies: 19 • Updated: 2026-06-28 22:38 UTC

OP

Well since it's becoming apparent all debt payers are suckers, what are the primary means of combating collapsing good goy score penalties resulting there-in-of?
Surely there are alternatives to getting things done without the threat of the bad good goy score stopping you.
>No Anon noooooooo you need to have a high good goy score! Get a credit card and start PUMPING it with debt IMMEDIATELY and then pay it off but not too fast so they can get some interest back! Do this for 20 years and then maybe you'll be able to pull out a mortgage!"

Haha nope, it's too late, all too fucked AND gay for that now.

Early replies (first 3)

62423651
>>62423592
Never took a loan, never had a credit card, never owed anyone anything. When the bank sees me depositing another 10k from oil shorts it begs me with ads to put it in some low-% scam account that will barely match inflation. Nope! Shit's out of my account by next Monday, cya suckers!

62423705
What did you expect to happen in an economy where no one can find a fucking job and the cost of living doesn't even remotely resemble what it was like in 2019?

You know what will help someone in this situation? Another five-round interview process where they still get a rejection email at the end.

62423715
>>62423705
Everyone can get a job lol the job reports are great only retards can’t get jobs

I literally just accepted a job offer after two weeks of looking

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62423727
>>62423715
>look how many brown assholes I had to lick to get accepted into slavery

62423731
>>62423727
Sorry to hear no one will hire you big guy, but it’s great that you’re trying!

Have you applied to SSI for your disability yet? Maybe your parents can help if the forms are too hard for you champ

62423736
>>62423592
>debt is BAD!
lol
NGMI

62423754
>>62423592
I stopped caring about my credit score. I stopped paying my credit card bills when I had to choose between those payments or keeping my car insurance and paying rent. They eventually sued me and after a long, stressful process that has profoundly altered my sense of trust in the American legal system and rule-of-law in practice (for the worse), I negotiated a 30% payment (though, with actual legal representation, I might have walked away scot-free). I'm still not convinced that these companies have standing to collect any alleged debt from me, but once you get branded with a judgment, you're fucked.

I wouldn't have had to take out the debt in the first place if I'd been paid properly, at the same rate as my parents had been at the same age and education level.

In general, my hope is that young people avoid the debt trap entirely, or else go so far in debt that becomes the creditors' issue. Eventually the chicanery has to end and either people start getting paid enough to live or the system collapses.

62423765
>>62423592
Is student loans a good way to make money?
I'm tired of working. I feel like going back to school and taking out a bunch of loans just to live off of.
I'll just stay in school taking loans until I'm ready to retire.

62423849
>>62423754
You took out the debt because you're stupid, full stop

The rest of your life will also suck because of that same truth

62423859
Do I really have to tap the sign?

62423868
>>62423754
The trick with this kind of shit is to just not have a mailing address. Bail on your cards in between a move or something next time so they can't actually hit you with anything. They sued you because they knew you were a good goy who would ply ball with them. You think they sue jamal and shanequa? Never play ball and ignore literally all mail from them if you can't afford to do some shit like this you cant afford to debt max

62423884
>>62423859
a quarter of Americans aren't paying it back. If they "Win" and collapse the system then I'm the sucker.
A Quarter of Americans is a big number, that's more than enough to win a civil war. The ability to "punish" that amount of people becomes less and less of a reality the more they grow.

62423906
>>62423651
based

62423909
>>62423592
>what are the primary means of combating collapsing good goy score penalties resulting there-in-of?
I have never been rich enough to move out of my mom's house and 80% of my credit card debt is on 0% APR until March 2028 cards. There is no incentive for me to not continue shifting the debt around forever until I become rich.

62423911
>>62423715
even mcdonalds wont give u an interview in 2026 its cooked unc.

62423923
>>62423868
this

just ignore it until they literally break down ur door, even if they come knocking pretend u arent home kek wtf are they gonna do

62423927
>>62423849
>You took out the debt because you were perennially underpaid and had living costs that exceeded pay despite cutting those costs judiciously, and spending carefully and sparingly in line with a carefully-planned budget.
That's right anon I'm surprised you know me so well.

62423950
>>62423868
>>62423923
Nah, they can get a judgment even if they never serve you notice that they're suing you - they just have to lie and say they did, and the default as far as the court is concerned is that it's accepted unless you challenge it, which you won't, because you didn't know you were being sued. AFAIK the only defense then is that you move to vacate the judgment within n days of it being entered because you weren't served, but I don't know if that actually works because the judge basically ignored everything I said to him. NLA.
That's the trick: when you walk into court, the judge is a lawyer and the debt collector is a lawyer and you are not a lawyer. Law? You think law rules? Those two rely on each other for their careers. You're just the mark.

62423989
>>62423592
It doesn't matter if your goyscore is rekt, you will not get a loan for a decent house. You might get enough to fix up a place but you'll be doing all the work. I make $52k a year in a really cheap city. Credit score is 814, no debt. Luckily my rent is around 16% of my salary. Saved up $40k for down payment. Banks won't lend me even $65k to buy a house. I don't earn enough. They'll let me buy an $80k truck at 17% though. Of course I put the savings into the market for now but I'm only really breaking even at this point.

item.title: WARNING: RIPPLE XRP ***EXTREME*** SUPER EMERGENCY!!! — 16 replies — 64.0/hr

OP

Insiders at Ripple officially speculate the XRP going to moon very high soon with the clarity act after the 4th of July, according to rumors, analysts say. This is huge and **MASSIVE** news for XRP and the cryptocurrency community as a whole.

You. Are. Not. Bullish. Enough.

Early replies (first 3)

62420069
>>62420066
shut the fuck up

62420100
>>62420066
XRP is a slush fund for banks. That is why they partnered with it.

62420110
So bullish

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62420119
Yes

62420157
Alright why is it pumping so hard?

62420174
>>62420157
It’s only up about 2% at the moment. Even 5% up isn’t much.

62420277
>>62420174

62420439
BOOOOOOOOOOOM

62420471
>>62420066
XRP died in 2024

62420479
>>62420066
I sold this scam the last pump attempt.

Thanks retards

>It died before that

62420531
>>62420066
HOLY FXCKING BOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOM

62420698

Fkn bullish

62420798

62421769
>>62420066

62423981
>>62420066

Best they can do a chainlink BUILD update where theyre removing it. Theyre probably going to remove staking now too

TRVST THV PLVN!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!

item.title: Reporting crypto to HMRC — 2 replies — 2.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
Reporting crypto to HMRC

Open thread • Replies: 2 • Updated: 2026-06-28 21:51 UTC

OP

Does anyone use koinly to report losses? If so does HMRC investigate and ask for evidence of losses (5-figures) or is the Koinly report enough?
Its good to report this as you can use it against capital gains (inc stocks) in the future.

Does anyone use a crypto accountant for reconciliation if trades number in the 100s?

Early replies (first 3)

62422994
I used chatgpt to help me file the most recent figures as my crypto accountants are charging £800!

62423807
HMRC? Who are they?!

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

No additional replies in the latest window.

item.title: VOO and Chill — 140 replies — 279.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
VOO and Chill

Open thread • Replies: 140 • Updated: 2026-06-28 22:08 UTC

OP

The more I look into investing and compare the historical returns of certain ETFs / individual stocks it seems like the whole philosophy of “VOO and Chill” is equal parts really smart and retarded
It’s like a no brainer for money but even now as long as the market is in a growth period shouldn’t you at least do QQQ?
It seems so simple that’d you miss a lot of upside but the returns are honestly not bad and extremely consistent
How accurate is this assessment? It’s both so simple and guaranteed that everyone should it but also too safe so you end up missing out on more upside
I’m talking 5-20 years investing here not trading

Early replies (first 3)

62400515
Boomer funds are gay and lame

62400554
>>62400515
What qualifies as one
Why
What crypto do you want to shill

62400560
boglehead method is the tried and tested way, buy the market with an index fund that has a low fee, invest monthly, do it consistently for decades and you WILL be rich
but biz/anons don't care about being rich when they're older, they want to be rich NOW
greed is a deadly sin

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62420887
>>62420075
Brevity is the soul of wit.

62421697
Anyone here going into DRAM

62421773
VOO and chill is the best because it's the most safe/leaning aggressive fund you can get. Alternatives:
>individual stocks: this always looks great in hindsight, but who the fuck knew that Micron was going to skyrocket? And if you picked Microsoft you would be underperforming VOO
>volatile ETFs like BAKA, DRAM, TQQQ: these are great when the market is up, but when you lose 15% in a few days you will feel it
>BTC, ETH: yeah right, the S&P beats these easily (inb4 "zoom out", nobody cares, that kind of increase will never be duplicated)

62421787
>>62420887
Brevity: Wit's soul

62421799
>"just VOO and chill bro"
>gets caught in a 5+ yr -60% drawdown event (2008) with no hedges to cover his balls
boyhood is regurgitating the VOO mantra
adulthood is meticulously engineering your poorfolio to get within 80% of index performance while cutting the drawdown periods by half

62421813
>>62400493
Or I can throw a good amount into Uranium miner indexes and chill to retire in half the time

62422318
>>62419913
>I even did completely mong shit like having a python script take a screenshot of every known stock loaded into eval sites like GuruFocus etc and then simply counting the green pixel ratio.
kek

62422375
>>62421799
ok Mr Buffett, what's an alternative long term investment strategy then?

62422392
>>62400493
Stock picking is still good and valid retard.
>But I'm losing % if I don't buy voo
This is smol brain thinking.

62422418
>>62422392
Im down about 100$ since starting my high risk portfolio a few weeks ago.
The low risk portfolios doing p good tho

62422766
>>62421799
Alright tell me the next big thing then
I see a lot of talk about the memory bottle neck happening through 2027/2028 is DRAM a safe bet

62422806
>>62422766
i use a modified barbell strategy that i call "dick and balls" method.

>40% high risk high return ETFs
this is your right nut and drives your explosive folio growth. you want trending sector or thematic ETFs here.

>40% value ETFs with low low beta
this is your left nut and provides slow, steady growth even during volatile conditions. stuff like SCHD would fall in here.

>20% ultrasafe assets like bonds and treasurys
this is your shaft, without which your balls have no raison d'etre. this will grow steadily as you grow older and provide the foundation upon which your entire portfolio stands erect.

when you're young, you take ballsy risks, hence the 40-40 balls.
as you get older, you seek wisdom and steady upwards thrust, hence your 20% shaft grows year by year.

use a portfolio backtester/optimizer to determine the exact composition of each component. you can get FAR less max drawdown than plain S&P while retaining most of returns. the right nut can be configured to catch seasonal upsides, i.e. whatever's hottest at the moment.

62422917
>>62422806
Okay but as someone with 55K sitting in VOO, just DCA each week, what should I look towards next
As Stated I was considering just beginning to go into DRAM for now and selling within 1 to 2 years

62422933
It's retarded unless you have like 2 million.
If you don't you have to take gambles.
Time is more important than money.

62422968
>>62422933
right but if you start young then you have time on your side so all you need to do is focus on making more money
it's an equation that always balances out, risk takes principal out of the equation and time

62422991
>>62421799
this is legitimately not true when compared to other indexes

62423039
>>62421799
>he thinks there will be another 08 event

62423048
>>62423039
have you seen the SP chart? Have you seen the state debt? have you seen the bond yield inversion?

62423061
>>62423039
>>62423048
Why does it matter if there is another 08 unless you plan to retire in 5-10 years

62423124
>>62422991
haha yeah

>>62423061
because your primary source of income would dry up under such conditions and you'll have to liquidate and partially realize your losses to pay your immediate obligations you chuckelfucking retard
>but i have cash reserves
really? enough to last you multiple years? i don't think so.

62423192
Are you a bad enough dude to hold and make it?

62423236
>>62423192
Is this a backtest or actual holdings in real time, I dont recognize the site UI

62423245
>>62423192
for reference, this is what Im rockin...

62423267
40/40/20 VDY/XEI/XEQT is extremely comfy for any Canadians reading this thread

62423719
>>62422917
retard he is telling you to risk adjust your allocations as you get older, when you're 60 it's more about capital preservation than getting high returns

62423769
this thread is getting dumber by the day

62423809
Using a reversion to the mean strategy on indexes, I managed to get a 17% CAGR over 54 years only trading indexes/ETFs (where data permits) on backtesting, significantly beating each individual index . More enquiry is warranted but I think "VOO and chill" can be improved upon and optimized.

62423821
>>62423719
he mentioned rn

62423875
>>62400636
It actually is something tho. $150 a month becomes 170k at 7% real returns for 30 years. This gives you an extra $570/mo in retirement.

Is that a LOT? Fuck no. But we’re talking poorfag standards where social security is the only thing you have. An extra 500-600 a month is a significant increase over base SS for a poorfag especially if you move to an ultra low cost of living area.

62423880
The reason i keep bringing up DRAM and other return stuff is because it's on my mind to go into that stuff now, sell, and then increase my VOO position even further - not to infinitely chase returns btw

item.title: Why aren't you holding BTC? — 102 replies — 91.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
Why aren't you holding BTC?

Open thread • Replies: 102 • Updated: 2026-06-28 21:32 UTC

OP

Too expensive? Prefer alts? Don't trust it? Or do you simply think there are better opportunities elsewhere?
Curious to hear your thoughts.

Early replies (first 3)

62395695
you can get better gains on stocks. those companies work for your money. btc sits there doing nothing but has the same risk profile as sharing a hobos needle without greater fools left to carry it to $200k

62395915
give me reasons to hold btc.

62395925
I am holding because I bought in 2020 before etfs now I refuse to sell because I'll get audit raped by the CRA supposedly so I'm stuck holding until it's got better tax laws or borrowing rates.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62415297
>>62396879
There is 'demand' for literally meme garbage like PEPE and DOGE which do nothing - not really an argument.

62415301
>>62415217
No, but MSTR collapse will cause a negative doom loop for Bitcorn if they have to start dumping.

62415492
>>62415228
Are you white?

62416132
>>62395655
I sold between 90-110k
Bought back between 59-64k
Still 1/3 of Tether left to buy further dips

62416334
>>62411213
Yeah. The Aegis partnership makes me even more interested to see how the TBV public testnet progresses.

62416338
>>62415301
but short term

62416355
>>62416132
Nice trades.

62416385
>>62395655
because I make money trading not holding garbage

62416439
>>62416385
Because I believe Bitcoin is the strongest long-term store of value in crypto. Short-term volatility doesn't change why I bought it in the first place.

62416488
>>62411265
I'm not holding bitcoin for next week. I'm holding it for the next decade.

62416497
>>62411213
I have doubts for Aave coin

62416506
Because every major dip has looked scary in the moment, but the long-term trend is what matters to me.

62416528
eistein coin

62417470
>>62416385
Garbage? Seriously?

62417484
>>62416497
Nobody's saying you have to like the coin. The point is the protocol and why it still dominates DeFi lending.

62417496
>>62411241
your choice

62417853
>>62415297
Memecoins are different

62419146
>>62395655
Because I'm not a retard

62419150
I know the maxis wont want to watch this…but btc is fundamentally flawed, as are all pow and pos consensus mechanisms…it’s not gonna work long term
https://m.youtube.com/watch?v=fo8ZScrXFZE&ra=m
Jesus loves you, Christ is King

62419226
>>62395655
I dont hold anything with zero value...maybe trade it

62419255
>>62419226
That's funny. From $1 to $100k and zero value?

62419275
>>62419255
Zero value as in speculative/doesnt generate a cashflow...sorry you know nothing about finance.

62420185
>>62419146
Non coiner. Ok

62421786
>>62419150
Hmm, that's your opinion.

62422222
>>62409106
>he doesnt realize 1 mcmansion is not enough and they need the price to go up into infinity
fucking bitcuck

62422228
Holy shit guys. sound the alarm bell. Someone just pointed out that managers in a company want to get paid for their efforts... No wonder why all stocks in the world are in a perpetual downtrend. We've finally figured it out, all thanks to a bitcuck. It was the damn managers ALL ALONG! using the stocks as a money printer. Better fucking buy some BITCOIN to get myself out of this mess. Lord what have I done? This market failure is finally solved by BITCOIN. Oh lord thank you lord. I have seen the light. Thank god for saving me from those evil damn managers who keep dumping their stocks on me. Trillions of liquidity, all harvested by CEOs... LET BANKS FALL! BITCOIN TO DA MOON!

62422277
>>62422228
So you just realized just NOW?

62422765
>>62419275
People only need x1000 so it doesn't matter.

62422776
>>62416506
??

62423752
>>62419226
How come?

OP

>According to leaked QQ chats: 'She failed a margin call' 'Flew a plane to ram the CITIC building.'


https://x.com/i/status/2070653653795709229

https://www.nst.com.my/world/world/2026/06/1474324/small-plane-crashes-beijing-skyscraper-pilot-killed-13-injured-watch

62420377

62420381
>>62420373

62420382
/biz/lets wouldn't even have the balls

62421137
>>62420366
"Suicide"

62421143
>>62420225
OK maybe these fishheads are absolutely fucking based after all.

62421187
>>62420225
It takes balls to kill yourself. Most women would just do some fake shit like cut themselves the wrong direction and post about it online to make money off of simps. This lady was brave and dedicated, I can respect that.

62421200
>>62420225
Based way to go out. Wonder if she knew about Skyking.

62421216
>>62420278
>I don't even know why even they would care to cover this up.
Stops it becoming a national pastime. This was basically going postal, but with no casualties other than herself. And Govts are not big on employees going Full Retard on the corporations, bad for morale.

62421367
>>62420225
basado and red china pilled

62421410
>>62420225
Pretty based for a woman ngl

62423614
Based af

62423625
>>62420265
I'll never understand suicidal thoughts like that.
At that point, it's those who would force you into slavery that are your enemy.

62423645
>>62420225
>She tried to escape.
>CCP goons crashed her.
>"Leaked" that it is a suicide.
communist playbook

62423655
>>62420242
Larry didn't have time to insure it

62423656
>>62423645
>>CCP goons crashed her.
How? Why would they waste so much effort and resources just to kill one person? The broker will get their money back either way they don't need the government's help for that shit.

62423664
>>62423625
>Gamble your life savings by putting all of it into a brokerage account as
>Economy is gonna economy, but the markets aren't always predictable
>Literally all of your money is gone

Gee I wonder why she would feel hopeless and think death is the only way out

62423667
>>62420247
But Chris Hansen on China Uncensored told me that all Chinese buildings are made of tofu and paper ...

62423686
>>62421187
Serious financial fuckups in China get executed anyway, so she was just sparing herself that humiliation and sending a message.

62423687
>>62423656
Do you know anything about china? The world does not have the same economic infrastructure as US or Europe

62423689
>>62423686
I find it fascinating that their government is it willing to just kill suits if they fuck up badly enough unlike in the states.

62423695
>>62423687
>Explains nothing

Let me ask again cuz why don't they bother killing one person? Explain to me why the feds would want that particular person 86ed for her I'll fuck up that at most only affects her, whoever manages the brokerage account she had access to, and whatever estate she was involved with (I'm assuming whatever that she owe prior to death will fall on that)

62423696
>>62423664
>Oh no suddenly I have NOTHING to lose taking more extreme risks to get wealth back, especially since I want to kill myself.
>Rather than taking this fearless potential to rebound my life, It's safer just to die.
Do you not see the stupidity here?

62423698
>>62423664
i invested all my money into link for the past 8 yers,

got liqd and lost all of it, about 250k...

i dont feel suicidal, i just moved back in with my mom...

im only 40, i can just be a truck driver now like the rest of my family. save up 200k in a decade and retire in a third world.

62423707
>>62423686
They only do that when there is widespread public harm to maintain the image of their fake communism.

62423718
>>62423696
Are you aware of how some brokerage accounts even work? You put money in the account as collateral (in this case it was likely her life savings or some other absurd amount of money) which essentially gives you permission to fuck around with a larger pool of money. You then use that to "hopefully" make better investment decisions to make more money. If you're an idiot however or you're just incredibly unlucky the value can sharply tank and your investments lose value which means the brokerage account owner loses money. They are going to cover their own ass no matter what which means it will use your collateral deposit as a safety net to soften the blow, hence her life savings getting drained basically instantly. Depending on what she did with the brokerage money and the agreement they have it's also possible she owed money even after her life savings got annihilated. It's often how amateur (sometimes even experience ones to be honest) day traders can lose money.

>>62423698
You also have to consider the different cultures as well. Given our current god-awful economy still living with your parents or moving back in with them after an adult isn't seen as THAT shameful anymore and in some cases it's even seen as financially smart. Eastern hemisphere Asians our status obsessed however so there are more willing to shame people or kill themselves for things we would consider as very minor. In China as dude for example if you're above a certain tax bracket you're considered worthless if you don't have a home because it's expected that you work your ass off and then some to impress both the girl AND her family. Their housing crisis is arguably even worse than ours because they hoard homes and apartment buildings like pokémon cards (look up "ghost cities" in China).

TLDR: your parents actually love you. That same treatment isn't guaranteed for the average asian person in the western hemisphere

62423730
>>62420247
So you're telling me jet fuel can't melt tofu dregs?

62423744
>>62423725
>The end result has been a combination of everyone lying about everything, such that the CCP doesn't even believe its own data/figures since people only ever tell their bosses what they want to hear and infinite passing the buck to someone else where they all finger point and throw each other under the bus anytime something goes wrong.
So not that much different than the US lol. Only difference is we (the US) actively protect and enable the dishonest rich fags. See the current "AGI in two more weeks AI's too dangerous for you lowly plebs" shit silicon valley pushed. That entire region needs to be glasses.

62423763
>>62423718
Yes that's cool and all but killing yourself over bankruptcy and not becoming more EXTREME as a result of it just shows you're probably a glownigger.

Pushing narratives about death before dishonor and not the will power of one with nothing to lose is very glownigger coded.
A suicide is harmless to others after all. Don't need to spend money on assassinating them.

62423784
>>62420242
Not enough jet fuel

62423826
>>62420225
Based foid. What the fuck Zhang gonna do when you have nothin' to lose???

item.title: >boss put me on a PIP 2 months ago >PIP requiernments is classical non sensical bullshit >dont even come close to fuflin… — 33 replies — 44.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
>boss put me on a PIP 2 months ago >PIP requiernments is classical non sensical bullshit >dont even come close to fuflin…

Open thread • Replies: 33 • Updated: 2026-06-28 21:54 UTC

OP

>boss put me on a PIP 2 months ago
>PIP requiernments is classical non sensical bullshit
>dont even come close to fuflining any of it anyways
>PIP ended last Tuesday
>haven't heard anything from anyone, still employed
Am I getting fired or not? I got a feeling im not out of the woods and they are just collecting paperwork. My boss said he had to talk to HR when i asked him about my PIP 2 weeks ago. The omly chace i have is that they were gonna fire me but deicded to keep me for a bit longer for some tax reason or something. I job hopped a lot and needed to last at this company for a few years to restore my reputation.. Really paranoid about losing it cuz im fucked if i do. Genuinely thinking of becoming a fetenoyl dealer beacuse being a career cuck is the worst torture imaginable

Early replies (first 3)

62423284
Most companies are downsizing so yeah, if they put you on PIP there's like a 99% chance they're just doing the legal mumbo jumbo so you can't sue after they fire you

62423286
>>62423284
Why haven't they fired me yet? Its fucked up that they are downsizing yet trying to make us feel like we arent worth dog shit and are chasing giant problems for the comapny by existing when we are really actually working hard the whole time

62423287
>>62423286
>Why haven't they fired me yet?
Like I said, companies need to go through a lot of legal mumbo jumbo so the fired employee can't sue them for wrongful termination

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62423291
>>62423287
But the PIP is over

62423311
You're fired you dolt

You will also always have this same problem at any job because of your mental

62423325
>>62423286
Email or slack your CEO and demand to know whether or not you're fired or not

62423329
i got put on a PIP two times at Microsoft and they didn't fire me

it's called toxic management, now that you know it just destroy the company from the inside

PS: i got laid off two years later, with my entire department

62423336
We have this thread like every week

62423359
>>62423278
Could be getting fired, could be getting mobbed, who knows. Start looking elsewhere just to be safe.

62423377
>>62423329
that's because you got siloed, dimwit.

62423379
>>62423359
OP should've started looking elsewhere a week before he got put on his PIP

62423385
>>62423325
Its a 40k person comapny, o dont think she will ever see the email

62423388
>>62423329
Why did they put u on a PIP though? I lost about 15 lbs from stress past two months. Mainly cuz I couldn't find another job, the job market is so fuckrd, I usually wouldn't care as it never took me more than 2 weeks to find a job. I was a hard worker until they did this. Its destroyed my summer and spring so far. Why would they do this???

62423395
>>62423336
Its probably all me kek. I dont know if I have a future or not and have no one to talk to
>>62423379
I tried, job market is fucked riggt now, never seen it this bad. Fake interviews for fake jobs..

62423397
>>62423388
u seem like a whiny irritating bitch

62423401
>>62423291
Doesn't matter. They might be "evaluating" your performance right now and fire you within a couple of weeks. They don't have to fire you while the PIP is active

62423404
>>62423388
for absolutely no reason, they were just using a pretext to put me on PIP every time

62423408
>>62423388
>>62423395
you're out of a job in 6 weeks tops. I hope you like flipping hamburgers bro

62423434
>>62423408
Don't people usually get fired at the end of a PIP though?

62423461
>>62423408
lmao as if, flipping burgers is also a luxury in this job market. He would be lucky to even get interviewed at mcdonalds desu

62423508
>>62423461
I have no problem getting interviews. Im very in demand in my field. Problem is that right now there are no real jobs. These are all ghost interviews for companies not planning on hiring anyone.

62423516
>>62423508
oh ur chilling if u get interviews then dude eventually u will get an offer.

62423531
>>62423516
Nope, its been like this for 4 months now. Never had no many interviews and so little luck. Its not luck or my skills. These are ghost jobs, they are never planning on hiring anyone. Look it up..

62423536
>>62423434
>Don't people usually get fired after a PIP
ftfy. And yes. But there's not really a time limit where they have to fire you within x days of the end of your PIP. The purpose of a PIP is to establish that you are unable to achieve acceptable performance levels, they can fire you at any time after that. It sounds like they're probably still interviewing your replacement.

62423543
>>62423531
Nobody goes through the expense of conducting interviews for ghost jobs though. You're just not good enough.

62423546
>>62423543
>Nobody goes through the expense of conducting interviews
you aren't familiar with HR roasties who need to justify their salary are you

62423552
>>62423546
For real. These women have nothing to do all day long and they love sitting in meetings because it makes them feel important.

62423557
>>62423543
>>62423546
He also isn't familiar with the fact that the HR roasites are actually helping their company by enabling keniving behavior like doing fake interviews so investory falsely think they are growing, excuses to hire overbroad or promote internallyfor 1/4th the prices cuz they legally have to try to hire someone in the US first or just general data gathering that they sell off to supplement their income. The HR roasitie probably pays for her salary and then some alone just form selling all the data..

62423561
>>62423543
Also, after this many interviews, its statistically impossible that its my fault at this point..

62423573
>>62423561
You are the common denominator in your repeated failures to get hired.

62423575
dude
they literally have scent receptors for Cluster A
it's your fault the way you were born

62423587
>>62423573
>muh common denominator
Ironically its the same problem who say
>causation doemst equal correlation

Unwilling to look deeper into subject and never took an actual probability class. Ghost jobs are everywere right now.
>>62423575
Im in engineering so idk why it would be a problem for them anyways.

62423819
>>62423278
The PIP shit is just so they have an excuse not to give you a raise.

item.title: Theyre ending the Chainlink BUILD program — 93 replies — 36.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
Theyre ending the Chainlink BUILD program

Open thread • Replies: 93 • Updated: 2026-06-28 20:07 UTC

OP

LMAO!!!!!

https://chain.link/blog/build-program-evolution

Early replies (first 3)

62417511
xrp is the chosen coin…it’s undeniable at this point

62417512
Come on man, this shit still spamming after 8 years? Just give it up. There are thousands of other coins.

62417514
"Chainlink Labs is transitioning from payments made in project tokens to focusing on commercial agreements involving fees paid in LINK or other liquid assets that can be readily converted to LINK."

Build members will buy my bags?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62421094
>>62418828
Based

62421109
>>62420922
"... or be converted to Link".

Another illiterate brown imbecile.

62421512
>>62421109
Nice so our $194.50 each combined total of rugged build shitcoin airdrops can go to purchasing an extra 20 link tokens for the reserve instead.. Marvelous!.................. KABOOOOOOOOM BULLISH CHAINLINK! $LINK #LINKTHEWORLD

62421528
>>62420922
What they're saying is that those token supplies have performed terribly and aren't even worth holding. Every single one of them. It's not a good look for the ecosystem, but the good thing is it won't affect the price because nothing does.

62421600
>>62421528
Not really. They're saying that projects aren't launching native tokens any more, and so aren't eligible for the current BUILD program. So they're finding different ways to encourage growth (particularly in the very small number of projects that actually succeed) while still making sure they PAY UP.

62421684
Chain Link Labs, "Our build coins aren't worth shit, so we're canceling build forever, thanks"!

62421956
>>62421600
The converted proceeds still should go directly towards the ecosystem participants (ie. NOPs and stakers), not to Chainlink Labs' own personal Reserve.
CLL should be adopting a more behind-the-curtains presence but instead they route the entire network through themselves. Not really neutral.

62422159
>>62420825
>It’s like you faggots think that these build rewards would make up half your profit from investing in link.

Thats how the turboshills hyped it up here.

Reserve, a flop.
Ccip revenue a flop
PAL a flop
Build rewards a flop
AMA a flop
Link staking 2.0 a flop
Eric schmidt, Tom szach, all flops

0 will flow to token holders , its all carrots. Only ones left are donkeys and the one on the cart.

62422217
>>62421109
Yes it won't happen. The projects can't afford link and their tokens have no value, so that means there just won't be any value coming into the ecosystem from it. Never was tbqh.

62422274
>>62421684
>>62421956
>>62422159
>>62422217

xerpies crying overtime again

62422290
>>62417503
Never mind, they were all dogshit anyway.
Require all transactions to be made in dollars and use that to fill the reserves and drive up the value of the token.

62422638
>>62421600
Ah that's better

>>62421956
And this is true. The reserve does nothing for us whereas at least the build rewards were supposed to go to us.

Does this announcement mean there is no build season 2 or subsequent though? That's what I took from. The announcement ie we get nothing now.

62422640
>>62422290
The reserve doesn't help holders, it only helps Chainlink the company

62422668
>>62422638
>The announcement ie we get nothing now.
another illterate brown xerpie

62422671
I am so glad I have never touched this piece of shit. The level of delusion here is unprecedented

62422754
>>62422671
You own XRP and you’re gay

62422761
>>62422671
baggie delusion doesn't bother me personally, it's the fact that shills were vehemently shilling and defending BUILD for years that infuriates me, and now they act like it was never a big deal
it's always 180 degree pivot and then denial and deflection, it's sickening

62422772
>>62422761
You've been reading shills defend Build for years? Why would you do that?

62423044
>>62422274
>never debate the point
>always ad hominem / adem / xerpie

62423259
>>62423044
>p-p-pls debate me!

62423262
Kek

62423276
>>62422761
Always been the case. Before it comes morons push whatever release as "they key" that will unlock all token value and you need to have your ducks in a row beforehand. Then it passes and they go fucking radio silent. Then half a year later that thing was never a big deal anyway and we should all not talk about it.

You had spastics making charts about how BUILD alone would return $1-2 per link staked each month. If you mentioned what a universal collection of utter vaporware shit the projects were you would get called a fudder.

62423282
>>62417535
Sir Gay indeed.

62423283
>>62423276
You're talking to yourself.
Nobody keeps tabs on every single granular development from a project they hate for years, but here you are juggling IDs pretending there are dozens of you.

62423312
>>62423276
My wife asked me where we're going to go on vacation this year with the money we made from the folk finance drop. I don't have the heart to tell her that we might be able to split a kids meal at Applebee's. She looks up to me as an intelligent head of the house.

62423353
60k link staked.. 20k USD a year... i needed the build rewards to fuck around with...

62423360
>>62423276
/thread
the constant gaslighting is even more annoying desu
>samefags reeeeeee

62423389
>>62423360
Why are you even reading this "gaslighting"? Who's forcing you to be here?

62423462
>>62423259
Hilarious deflection after doing this
>>62422274

The absolute state

62423474
>>62423353
How does 2 million a year sound?

item.title: UNDER $10K NET WORTH THREAD #1 — 170 replies — 31.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
UNDER $10K NET WORTH THREAD #1

Open thread • Replies: 170 • Updated: 2026-06-28 17:09 UTC

OP

Brothers,

SpaceX has begun to rise. Its success or fall is of little importance to us in the short term.

In the meanwhile we must devise ways to gain a spot at the table with our kin in the six figure club.

I for one propose we spend our time learning a subject. Knowledge is the greatest wealth of all.

However, I would be remiss to not tell the truth that I haven't a clue what we should study.

So instead I suggest we create an eggregore for these threads. Others made monero-chan, bobo and sminem

I am thinking we create one based on a girl who would be willing to believe in us while we are poor and brave the pain throughout having to witness our more disappointing moments of action. Never losing faith in us, she is always there. Yes. This is how we shall spend our time.

At any rate, that is my suggestion for tonight's fun. I have to work in the morning so let's not get too rowdy. Take it or leave it. If you will take it, then be sure to double it and pass it on to the next anon.

Let us commence the general!

Come one, come all!

Early replies (first 3)

62373772
>>62373752
Jannies will surely delete these threads. Instead of creating an eggregore why not try something new and usurp an old one. It may be a derivative of one which has already been made.

62373798
>>62373772
Hello anon thanks for joining. You are poster #1. Your anime image shall be the eggregore. The "coffee" in the image is rather a good omen. We all know images with hot girls accompanied by a brief post on coffee attracts all the attention.

Now that matter is settled, help me add some links to this post here. This will be the header for the start of each general

Helpful links

>Educational materials
https://w9.kaijimanga.com/

>Looking to try the TCG route?
https://youtube.com/@alphainvestments69?si=tDAPjnRSZhyCIGP5

>Lost all hope?
https://www.indeed.com/


>Hidden in plain sight
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Limitless_(film)

>Games to strengthen you financial acumen
https://mahjongsoul.yo-star.com/

>Learn your from your betters
https://ia600609.us.archive.org/3/items/the-house-of-rothschild-ferguson-niall/The%20House%20of%20Rothschild%20-%20Ferguson%2C%20Niall.pdf
What is the name of that character in your post?

62373799
>>62373772
verily, a true fact

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62403337
>>62401765
This general also is a work in progress, I am hoping 10 generals from now we have a much better work flow, plan and ultimately success. In some capacity.

62404260
Bump

62404574
No point in this general. Fastest way to make 10k is just to work for a bit, you shouldn’t be investing

62404709
Bump

62404737
>>62373752
its a spacex thread?
its time to buy o better buy in few days one week

62405715
bump

62407354
Page 8 bump, /biz/ must be losing money again because there's an uptick in activity.

62407450
almost 9 months since i started my first job after college
this feels stagnant and joyless
what exactly do i need to do to escape wageslavery and poverty

62407931
Someone send me ten grand so I can join the club, thanks

62408558
>>62407450
What were you doing in 2017, 2021 and December of 2024?

62408565
>>62407354
Thank you, my wage job prevents me from dedicating my energies to this thread but I should have some time soon

62408611
>>62408584
Wtf are you on about dude, yes I made 54 posts over the span of 8 days. I'm surprised you're not taking your own advice and leaving 4chan.

62410044
Bump

62410051
Under 10k you just need to have a job and save cash.
You need cash flow and if you dont have shit a job is the only way to start.

62410731
Bump

62411520
>>62408558
>2017
still in high school
>2021
still in uni
>December of 2024
just graduated and was looking for jobs (i still had 0 dollars to my name at that point)

62411614
>>62373752
>>62373772
>>62373798
>>62373799
>>62373803
>post Maccari's "Cicero Denounces Catiline" (1888)
>crop out Catiline
Fuck you OP, I'm glad you're poor.

62412314
>>62411520
You could've played the memecoin casino in 2021.

62413842
Bump, I'll be free tomorrow

62416357
Bump

62417812
page 9 bump. Don't say I never did anything for you.

62418065
Almost the weekend then I can devote my energies to this thread.

And a big thank you to the poster above this post, looks like you were based after all

62418133
Bump

62418220
I got out and made tens of dollars this week with MU and SNDK. Lost some with WDC.

62419326
This is very stupid but an anon in some other thread weeks ago shilled me TNYA. He gave no explanation, no catalyst, no description what the company even does but he said that we’ll be eating good in like 1-2 years I don’t even remember exactly when. But hey it’s a cheap stock and he sounded confident to me

62419390
go all in hbar when it bottoms at 0.03

62419964
Bump, just woke up, let's get started

62420874
It would appear that everyone is talking about the new WENDYs Stock

Naturally we should take some time to consider this potential moonshot. Could be a waste of time. In a few I will post information I find relevant to the stock and whether investing is the right option

62422903
Another page 9 bump

62423064
Good to see the thread is still going strong. Today is my off day, let's look into the next subject "predictive programming"

We'll start with Oliver tree

item.title: Missed yet another one — 13 replies — 18.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
Missed yet another one

Open thread • Replies: 13 • Updated: 2026-06-28 21:55 UTC

OP

Knew about it at fucking $100k market cap but dismissed it as another stupid scam rug. Low and behold it goes 1000x. Could've become a millionaire and escaped this stupid space forever. Just fuck everything and most especially fuck the stupid X+SOL ecosystem.

Early replies (first 3)

62422604
brutal

62422608
>>62422583
Saaarbenchodbasterdblody look at this organic wyt dev pump and be the 500$ exit liquidity madarchod!

62422619
>>62422583
Btw its 99% own money hunting 1% fomo, so it would not pump this hard if u had put in 500$, he would rug you way before.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62422626
>>62422619
Damn. Even the scam rugpullers are struggling to find liquidity.

62422679
>>62422583
1.2% liquidity wow. And insider supply? Man /biz/ we need to pump Foodpepe to 10m to finally win again againt the cabal.

62422847
This is what I hate about this faggot ass board.
I get it the gay captchas are necessary to stop bots and jeets but shitcoin threads shouldn't be deleted.
Everyone is an adult here and the decision to buy or not something it's up to each individual.
BRING BACK SHITCOIN GENERALS

62423214
>>62422847
Nigger it has 1% liquidity, literal kumar rugpull madarchod!

62423260
>>62422847
Here is you answer.

No faggot.

62423264
>>62422583
You have what is called a negativity bias.

You forget the hundreds of rugs you avoided, while hyperfocusing on the few things you missed out on. You're not a psychic so stop trying to think like one

62423347
>>62423214
It's scamlana
every shitter is a low liquidity asset
we can just go back to shill ETH plays like the old times, you know?

62423685
>>62423347
Shitlana needs to unironically die. The fucking billion rugpulls made by useless retards every single fucking hour killed the alt season.
No one is taking crypto seriously when shitlana exists.

62423716
>>62423685
>>62423347
That means they're doing something right, don't act as if other chains are holier than thou they're all useless and serve nothing but to give an avenue for gamblers to be complete degenerates.

62423830
>>62422583
>$40m volume wash trading
Imagine having this kind of money and still trying to scam a bunch of poorfags out of $50 lol

item.title: Another stablecoin just RUGGED — 44 replies — 14.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
Another stablecoin just RUGGED

Open thread • Replies: 44 • Updated: 2026-06-28 19:32 UTC

OP

MIM (Magic Internet Money) stablecoin just rugged.
So many stablecoins are rugging right now it's crazy

Early replies (first 3)

62408562
IT has just begun

62408566
magic internet money still exists?

62408598
>>62408557
amazing they're even still around

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62414728
so depending on the collat that they start hiking rates on... this might re-peg
might

62414746
most likely won't re-peg
>reason:
their depreciated cauldrons is where most of the dogshit collat likely is
even if liquidated, they might not cover for a full repeg

62414751
>>62414664
Why would I want to repay my debt?

62414752
>>62414655
>manually approve more emissions via multisig
LMAO

62414758
>>62414751
dudes who borrowed from this might be better off letting liquidation happen than getting their dogshit collat back
only God knows what fuckery might be in the "depreciated cauldrons"

62414805
>>62414664
I will not be buying any but if you buy and it repairs, sell it the second it does.
>>62414718
>Buying bitcoin
Lmfao Buy real coins with actual utility.

62414828
>>62414805
I won't buy, this shit has thin liquidity
people might panic and send it to fractions of a cent just for the fuck of it
of course, at that point everyone is going to crowd and buy
but right now, it just looks like something went structurally wrong
and by what that guy posted, considering this shit is managed by fucking multisigs that put rates manually into the contracts...
it kinda does

62414831
>>62414805
>if you buy and it repairs

How’s the weather in Bangladesh?

62414841
>>62414831
Reread what I wrote retard. I said I will not buy it but encouraged him to not baghold the exit repeg if it happens because it never recovers and stays. You are so cooked by doom scrolling reels it is insane. You are incapable of reading a single sentence with patience for comprehension.

62414846
>>62414828

62416862
>>62414658
Did the people behind MIM play a role in that? I thought MIM was just used (among several other stablecoins) as an attack vector on Curve to de-peg UST. Which we later found out (through a lawsuit) was allegedly done by Jane Street.

Unless I'm missing something then blaming the MIM creators for it is the same as blaming the USDT and USDC creators, which makes no sense

62416965
>>62416862
This is the MIM marketcap. It spiked this high because of the UST "degenbox" strategy that took advantage of Terra's fixed high yields. See that first blip in January 2022? That was in reaction to the collapse of the Wonderland (TIME) ponzi scheme, which was run by the same people as MIM. This was the beginning of the end for Terra, as liquidity rapidly pulled out, Do Kwon would then make a fatal error when he failed to understand that billions of dollars could be withdrawn on a hair trigger. Is MIM solely responsible for Terraluna's collapse? No... but they were definitely in the thick of it
